title: phoradendron serotinum subsp. tomentosum
intro: Phoradendron serotinum subsp. tomentosum, also known as the eastern mistletoe, is a parasitic plant that grows on the branches of deciduous trees, particularly oak and maple. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been used to treat various health conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Phoradendron serotinum subsp. tomentosum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
